title: auth — subprocessors
description: Auth invokes two kinds of subprocessors. KMS providers for JWT signing key envelope-encryption (invoked on every sign / verify — the KMS-decrypted plaintext is in-memory-only). GeoIP provider for the new-location detection listener (invoked on every login for the geolocation_hint used in notification payloads).

subprocessors.name: AWS KMS
subprocessors.purpose: Envelope encryption of JwtSigningKey.secret_encrypted. Every JWT signing operation KMS-decrypts the row's ciphertext under the current data key into a stack-scoped variable; never held in a process-scoped cache.
subprocessors.location: customer's AWS region (default us-east-1; configurable per deployment)
subprocessors.when_engaged: auth.jwt.algorithm = 'HS256' AND signing key uses KMS backend
subprocessors.notes: Same KMS backend as identity/mfa_secret_encrypted for operational simplicity. Wave 1c may switch to per-Application KMS keys for tenant-isolation guarantees.

subprocessors.name: HashiCorp Vault
subprocessors.purpose: Envelope-encryption alternative to KMS for on-prem deployments. Same operational contract.

subprocessors.name: MaxMind GeoIP2
subprocessors.purpose: Resolve request IPs to (city, country) for the LoginFromNewLocationNotification's geolocation_hint. Never used for authorization decisions — only for user-facing notification copy.
subprocessors.location: MaxMind on-premises DB shipped with the service (no outbound call to MaxMind's servers at request time)
subprocessors.when_engaged: auth.session.detect_new_device=true AND auth.new_location_login category is on for the User
subprocessors.notes: The GeoIP database is licensed + updated weekly. IP-to-location resolution happens LOCALLY (no outbound call at request time), so this is a subprocessor of the DATABASE, not the RUNTIME. Notifications carry only city+country; the raw IP never appears in the notification body.

not_a_subprocessor: firebase/php-jwt — in-process library, no data leaves the app
OpenTelemetry / metrics collector — receives aggregate metrics only (no identifiers)
Email delivery (SES / Postmark / SMTP relay) — handled by notifications module; auth doesn't maintain its own delivery pipeline
haveibeenpwned — owned by identity substrate's strong_password rule; auth composes but doesn't invoke directly
